We are a growing Partnership Firm in Kothrud, Pune, specializing in Accounting, Taxation, and Auditing. We are looking for a dedicated Semi-Qualified CA (CA Inter) to join our team and handle diverse assignments.

Key Responsibilities:
• End-to-end Accounting and Finalization of Accounts.
• Statutory & Internal Audits (including Trust Audits and Tax Audits).
• Compliance management for GST and Income Tax.
• International Taxation (15CA/CB & Equalisation Levy)
• Virtual CFO Services (MIS, Budgeting & Cash Flow)
• Labor Law & ROC Compliances

Candidate Profile:
• Appaeated for CA Inter or CA Final .
• Prior experience in Finalization and Audit at traditonal CA practice Firm is preferred .
• Strong understanding of Tax compliance.

Work-Life & Growth:
• Location: Paud Road, Kothrud, Pune.
• CA Final Leaves: We support your professional growth;
study leaves will be granted based on mutual understanding.

Work Location = Pune

Job Opening

Position & Vacancies = Article Assistant - CA (05 Open Positions)

• Expected to be part of team carrying out audit engagements for the medium-large corporates independently
• Expected to exercise compliance with Audit manual, Auditing Standards and legal regulations
• Expected to work closely with the Partner/Manger to ensure quality of client engagements.

About DRPCA (Peer Reviewed Firm)

Since 1995, DRP & Co. LLP has been helping corporates improve performance through effective risk and financial management. We offer solutions in audit & assurance, corporate governance & compliance, Tax advisory & regulatory services. Our team of 35+ qualified staff is headed by our leadership team, having diversified expertise.

We at DRP provide support to our clients by constantly exploring the means for the adoption of compliant business practices & supports them in the development of integrated control systems. We strongly believe that compliance and integrated systems are core for building sustainable businesses.

Our clientele includes large corporates, Multi-National Companies, & financial institutions from sectors like real estate, sugar, distillery, renewable energy, power, auto components, FMCG, IT, EPC, Pharma, Electronics, Paper etc.

We like challenges & we choose to be always ready through the code of conduct we adapt to serve our clients. How do we do it? Three principles always guide us while we serve our clients -
• Continuous learning - We make efforts to learn from our clients' businesses to experience and understand the practical needs of our clients.

We prefer being engaged for value-added and target-based assurance or advisory services to suit our client's unique requirements. We are ready for inclusive mandates & work with existing advisory teams due to our focus on promoting integrated systems for our clients. We enjoy our cameo plays.

Continuous engagements with a client help us understand the advantages or disadvantages of existing systems. We prefer working on strengthening advantages without bulldozing our ways of service deliveries.

PRM BU Lead – FP&A

Sr Analyst, Financial Planning & Analysis

Your Future Evolves Here

Evolent Health has a bold mission to change the health of the nation by changing the way health care is delivered. Our pursuit of this mission is the driving force that brings us to work each day. We believe in embracing new ideas, challenging ourselves and failing forward. We respect and celebrate individual talents and team wins. We have fun while working hard and Evolenteers often make a difference working in everything from scrubs to jeans.

Are we growing? Absolutely and Globally. In 2021 we grew our teams by almost 50% and continue to grow even more in 2022. Are we recognized as a company you are supported by for your career and growth, and a great place to work? Definitely. Evolent Health International (Pune, India) has been certified as “Great Places to Work” in 2021. In 2020 and 2021 Evolent in the U.S. was both named Best Company for Women to Advance list by Parity.org and earned a perfect score on the Human Rights Campaign (HRC) Foundation’s Corporate Equality Index (CEI). This index is the nation's foremost benchmarking survey and report measuring corporate policies and practices related to LGBTQ+ workplace equality.

We recognize employees that live our values, give back to our communities each year, and are champions for bringing our whole selves to work each day. If you’re looking for a place where your work can be personally and professionally rewarding, don’t just join a company with a mission. Join a mission with a company behind it.

What You’ll Be Doing:

Job Description: Senior Analyst, Strategic Finance

Position Overview: We are seeking a Senior Analyst for our Strategic Finance team to support the Product and Technology Business Line. This role will support the company's financial planning, forecasting, budgeting, and reporting functions. The Senior FP&A Analyst will work closely with Accounting, Procurement, and department heads to provide insights and financial analysis that drive business decisions. The ideal candidate will have experience in managing monthly accruals, accounting processes, procurement processes, and will possess strong analytical and problem-solving abilities to drive business performance.

Responsibilities:

Support the Finance team by executing key deliverables below with excellent attention to detail:

· Work closely with the accounting team to ensure proper documentation and recording of accruals, journal entries, and other financial transactions (e.g., capital project codes, spend category, cost center, etc.).
• Provide financial oversight of procurement activities and ensure proper accounting treatment of purchasing and vendor transactions
• Maintain and update existing standard models (e.g., staffing, detailed budget builds, etc.) as well as contribute to further development of those models related to new initiatives (e.g., new strategic initiatives, staff versus contractor evaluation, CapEx strategy, etc.)
• Manage the monthly accrual process, ensuring accurate recognition of expenses in accordance with accounting principles and company policies.

· Assist with month-end and year-end closing activities to ensure timely and accurate financial reporting.
• Support monthly, quarterly, and annual reporting and budgeting cycles with KPIs, and insights tailored to Technology and Product leadership. Including: developing cost center budgets, capital planning, and project-level forecasting for Technology investments and Product initiatives.
• Track department-level performance (supporting business line leaders with monthly reports headcount, spend, variance analysis, department P&L forecasting)
• Maintain, create, automate, and improve Adaptive Insights financial models and reporting templates, focused on the evolving needs of the Technology and Product business area
• Liaise with other teams under the Finance umbrella (e.g., Accounting) to maintain accurate forecasting and partner on month-end variance analyses to better refine monthly forecasting
• Support special projects, including ROI analysis for new Technology investments, Product launches, and platform enhancements.

The Experience We Prefer:
• Chartered Accountant/CPA, CMA or Master’s degree in Business Finance or equivalent experience
• 3-5 years of experience as an Analyst or Associate in corporate finance (FP&A), investment banking, or other Finance role with financial modeling experience and ideally coming from an institution with a training / rotational / development program
• Proactive problem-solving skills with the ability to work independently
• Ability to take initiative while managing time across multiple workstreams and stakeholders
• Experience working with Accounts Payable, Accounting, and Procurement teams for invoice, Purchase Order, and accrual related items
• Extensive experience using Microsoft Excel
• Strong analytical abilities, with a track record of identifying insights from financial information and translating into business analytics and recommendations with a high level of accuracy
• Experience with ERP systems (e.g., Workday, SAP, Oracle) and FP&A tools (e.g., Hyperion, Adaptive Insights, Office Connect) is preferred.

To ensure a secure hiring process we have implemented several identity verification steps, including submission of a government issued photo ID. We conduct identity verification during interviews, and final interviews may require onsite attendance. All candidates must complete a comprehensive background check, in-person I-9 verification, and may be subject to drug screening prior to employment. The use of artificial intelligence tools during interviews is prohibited and monitored. Misrepresentation will result in immediate disqualification from consideration.

Mandatory Requirements:

Employees must have a high-speed broadband internet connection with a minimum speed of 50 Mbps and the ability to set up a wired connection to their home network to ensure effective remote work. These requirements may be updated as needed by the business.

​

Evolent Health is an equal opportunity employer and considers all qualified applicants equally without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran status, or disability status.

Senior Level

data.job_title: Senior Accountant - Lease Accounting and Financial Reporting
data.job_description:
Senior Accountant - Lease
The Opportunity:
Assist in the preparation of external and internal financial reporting including all periodic Securities & Exchange Commission (“SEC”) filings. Work closely with different functions including Corporate Consolidations, Global Finance, Legal, Tax, Treasury and internal and external auditors. Facilitate the information flow from all subsidiaries and the process of preparing SEC filings. Assist with improvements to financial reporting processes.

This role is responsible for supporting core corporate accounting activities, including lease accounting under ASC 842, stock‑based compensation accounting under ASC 718, monthly close processes, and coordination with global teams. The position requires strong analytical abilities, attention to detail, and effective cross‑functional collaboration.
Major Responsibilities
Lease Accounting & Fixed Assets
• Enter new leases and update lease changes in the Lease Query Tool.
• Prepare and record monthly journal entries and payment reconciliations in alignment with ASC 842.
• Collaborate with global teams to support centralized lease accounting processes.
Stock‑Based Compensation
• Prepare and analyze stock‑based compensation expenses in accordance with ASC 718.
• Support quarterly disclosure requirements for 10Q/10K filings.
Month-End Close & Reporting
• Support the monthly close process, including preparation and review of journal entries, account reconciliations, analytical reviews, and documentation.
• Prepare monthly results for reporting into the data mapping tool for consolidated financials.
• Assist Financial Reporting and Consolidation teams with close activities and data needs.
Business Partnership & Collaboration
• Build strong working relationships with finance leaders and cross‑functional teams.
• Develop a solid understanding of financial processes, inter‑department data flows, reporting needs, and ongoing initiatives.
• Communicate insights clearly and responsibly to the Controller’s Group.
Audit Support
• Support quarterly internal and external audit processes with accurate, compliant documentation.
Process Improvement
• Continuously evaluate existing processes to identify opportunities to improve efficiency, accessibility of financial data, and data integrity.
Ad‑hoc Support
• Perform ad‑hoc financial and economic analyses as requested.
• Contribute to special projects and other duties as assigned.
Qualifications
Education & Experience
• CA Intermediate with 3–6 years of relevant experience, or
• CA with 1–2 years of experience in Record to Report
• Demonstrated experience with technical accounting topics such as Lease Accounting and Stock‑Based Compensation.
• Proficiency in Microsoft Excel required; experience with SAP is mandatory.
Knowledge, Skills & Abilities
• Strong understanding of U.S. GAAP, IFRS, and corporate accounting policies.
• Familiarity with Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) requirements, with experience applying relevant controls.
• Ability to prepare, interpret, and analyze financial statements, including P&L, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow.
• Strong written and verbal communication skills to effectively collaborate with multiple teams and stakeholders.
• Strong analytical mindset, organizational skills, and the ability to prioritize and manage multiple tasks.
• Proactive, detail‑oriented, and committed to continuous learning.

About the Role:

Zywave, Inc., a leading Insurtech SaaS provider is looking to add a Pune, India based Senior Accountant to its global corporate accounting team. The Senior Accountant is responsible for executing and improving general ledger accounting, the month-end close processes, account reconciliations, and financial analysis across multiple entities and currencies. This role ensures compliance with US GAAP, supports tax and audit activities, and drives process improvements, including automation initiates using AI-powered tools.

Key Responsibilities
• Manage and execute timely and accurate month-end and year-end close tasks, including journal entries, account reconciliations and variance analysis.
• Serve as the subject matter expert in technical accounting areas, including but not limited to Commissions, Leases, Fixed Assets, Prepaids, and Intercompany.
• Work closely with Finance, Operations, Legal, HR, and Sales to ensure accurate financial reporting.
• Identify and implement process improvements, including automation using AI-powered tools and bots.
• Ensure compliance with internal controls and statutory accounting guidance.

Essential Tasks:

Commissions Accounting: Partner with HR and Sales Operations to understand commission plans and ensure proper accounting treatment. Manage capitalized commission accounting, including identifying eligible costs, calculating amortization schedules, and maintaining supporting schedules and documentation. Record monthly commissions earned expense and reconcile related balance sheet accounts.

Lease Accounting: Manage end to end lease accounting in accordance with ASC 842, including classification, initial recognition, and ongoing measurement. Prepare monthly lease journal entries, reconciliations, and supporting documentation.

Fixed Assets: Oversee the fixed asset lifecycle, including capitalization, disposals, transfers, and depreciation. Maintain the fixed asset register and perform monthly reconciliations of fixed asset accounts and depreciation expense.

Prepaid Expenses: Manage the prepaid expense schedule and ensure timely amortization of balances. Review vendor invoices to determine proper prepaid treatment and coding. Prepare monthly journal entries and reconciliations for all prepaid accounts. Collaborate with department owners to validate timing and accuracy of prepaid allocations.

Other Tasks: Perform other assigned tasks which may include expense accruals, intercompany variance reconciliations, cash transactions, and other continuous improvement and strategic projects

Qualifications:
• Bachelor of Commerce (B. Com), specifically in Accounting & Finance (BAF) or USA bachelor’s degree in accounting.
• Indian Chartered Accountancy (CA) or USA Certified Public Accountant (CPA) preferred.
• Strong understanding of US GAAP.
• 3+ years of professional corporate accounting experience.
• Experience with month-end close including journal entries preparation, account reconciliations, and analysis.
• Excellent analytical, communication, and problem-solving skills.
• Advanced Microsoft Excel skills (e.g. pivot tables, VLOOKUP, macros).

Proficiency with the following systems is a plus:
• Sage Intacct
• Microsoft Office & Related Tools (e.g. Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Teams, Outlook, Power BI)
• Experience with AI and automation tools (e.g. Microsoft Copilot)

Why Zywave?

Zywave is a United States based company with operations in Pune India. Zywave empowers insurers and brokers to drive profitable growth and thrive in today’s escalating risk landscape. Only Zywave delivers a powerful Performance Multiplier, bringing together transformative, ecosystem-wide capabilities to amplify impact across data, processes, people, and customer experiences. More than 15,000 insurers, MGAs, agencies, and brokerages trust Zywave to sharpen risk assessment, strengthen client relationships, and enhance operations. Additional information can be found at www.zywave.com.

How to Apply

If you meet the above qualifications and are excited about the opportunity, please email your updated resume to Anindita.Acharjee@zywave.com with your availability and salary expectations. We will connect with you shortly regarding your fitment for the role.

About the Role:

The Supply Chain Risk Analyst will be responsible for identifying, assessing, and managing risks associated with third parties. The analyst will work closely with various departments and stakeholders to develop and implement effective risk management strategies. Additionally, the analyst will be responsible for understanding and mitigating Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) risks within the supply chain. This includes evaluating the environmental impact of suppliers, ensuring fair labor practices, and maintaining compliance with governance standards.

Responsibilities:
• Risk Assessment: Assess risks including Geographical, Reputational, Cyber Security, Sanctions, Human Resources, Business Continuity, Anti-Bribery/Anti-Corruption, Artificial Intelligence, ESG, and Privacy.
• Daily Operations: Perform day-to-day responsibilities of the SCRM program, including intake and handling of requests for new third parties, gathering documentation for due diligence and onboarding tasks, and maintaining the SCRM database.
• Monitoring: Monitor third parties for changes in their risk profile, including mergers and acquisitions, regulatory changes, and other external factors.
• Risk Assessment: Conduct risk assessments on new and existing third parties.
• Database Maintenance: Ensure the integrity of the SCRM database by accurately documenting and consistently updating contact information, products, and services to maintain reliable and trustworthy data.
• Reporting: Assist in generating standardized reports to inform business owners and management on third-party risk and performance.
• Risk Management: Encourage and enable effective risk management change.
• Support: Provide support and expertise to business owners on third-party relationship management.
• Incident Management: Conduct ongoing management of escalations and incidents related to due diligence and monitoring of third parties.
• Remediation: Facilitate or assist in the remediation of any third-party-related findings or issues.
• Program Improvement: Contribute to the improvement of the Supply Chain Risk Management Program.
• Vendor Communication: Communicate with third-party vendors to address identified risks and ensure compliance with contractual obligations and regulatory requirements.
• Industry Trends: Stay abreast of industry trends and best practices related to Supply Chain Risk Management.
• Special Projects: Assist with any special projects as needed.
• Bachelor’s degree in Information Security, Business Administration, or a related field.
• Minimum of 2 years of experience in third-party risk

Our Interview Practices

To maintain a fair and genuine hiring process, we kindly ask that all candidates participate in interviews without the assistance of AI tools or external prompts. Our interview process is designed to assess your individual skills, experiences, and communication style. We value authenticity and want to ensure we’re getting to know you—not a digital assistant. To help maintain this integrity, we ask to remove virtual backgrounds and include in-person interviews in our hiring process. Please note that use of AI-generated responses or third-party support during interviews will be grounds for disqualification from the recruitment process.

Applicants may be required to appear onsite at a Wolters Kluwer office as part of the recruitment process.
